name: discovery_and_llm_fallback_or_handoff
purpose: Handle requests that do not match the current AI indexes by inspecting the codebase, asking a local Ollama model for structured discovery, or producing a short handoff prompt for GPT or Claude.

related_ext_files.role: Builds a lightweight snapshot of controllers, services, entities, and pages from the current codebase.
Generates strict JSON discovery prompts, resolves local Ollama models, and creates compact GPT or Claude handoff prompts.
Chooses between Ollama discovery and manual handoff when no indexed feature matches.

notes: The Ollama request path enforces JSON output so the discovery response can be parsed safely.
If Ollama is down or no model can be resolved, the helper prints a short prompt that can be pasted into GPT or Claude.

name: feature_context_selection_and_prompt_generation
purpose: Detect the most relevant business feature group for a request, load the matching context bundle, and produce a prompt plus execution plan for downstream AI agents.

related_ext_files.role: Ranks business features using feature metadata, learned keywords, entry points, and core entities.
Builds a service-aware context bundle containing features, flows, integrations, and override paths.
Builds the final prompt and the structured prompt-rewrite variant for AI agents.
Creates deterministic execution steps from the selected feature context and detected intent.
Classifies the request into planning styles like investigate, repair, validate, or implement.

notes: This is the primary happy-path feature for known business features already represented under /ai.
The output is structured so multiple AI agents can consume the same result consistently.

name: feedback_and_adaptive_learning
purpose: Persist feedback, learned keywords, and intent adjustments so the helper can improve future feature detection and prompt selection across repeated use.

related_ext_files.role: Stores feedback summaries, learned keywords, and rolling prompt acceptance data.
Uses learned intent weights to refine future planning-style detection.
Uses learned feature weights to improve feature ranking.
Provides the base intent configuration that learning builds on top of.

notes: Learning data can live in the target project when that project has an ai/system directory, otherwise the helper falls back to its own shared system folder.
This feature improves routing quality without changing the underlying business features directly.

name: knowledge_base_generation_and_self_healing
purpose: Generate and update the AI knowledge base under /ai so the helper can persist new business features, flows, and indexes instead of relying only on runtime discovery.

related_ext_files.role: Validates discovery results and writes new feature and flow JSON files without overwriting existing knowledge.
Provides safe JSON load and write helpers used to update the AI indexes.
Defines the project kickoff workflow that leads from discovery to KB generation.
Captures the delivery order and constraints for building a reusable project knowledge base.

notes: Write-back is intentionally gated by confidence and existence checks to avoid low-quality KB mutations.
This feature turns one-off discovery into durable project knowledge.

name: target_project_resolution_and_service_discovery
purpose: Resolve the active project root, discover one or more AI-enabled services, and normalize index access so the helper can analyze the correct repository context.

related_ext_files.role: Sets the effective target root from the current shell directory and dispatches the analyze command by default.
Resolves the active project root, scans for AI-enabled services, and normalizes index file loading.
Passes project-root settings through the CLI into the runtime analysis flow.

notes: The helper can analyze itself or another repository depending on the current working directory or explicit project-root override.
Index loading supports both keyed JSON objects and raw JSON arrays.
